name: FinCEN News and Advisories RSS
contamination_reasoning: Live government news feed for financial crimes enforcement advisories. Different from OFAC sanctions (already wired). Future advisories don't exist yet. Not a standard TSFM dataset — event-driven regulatory data.
difficulty_note: Advisory publication rate is driven by regulatory cycles, geopolitical events, and emerging financial crime typologies. Non-stationary with regime shifts following major legislation (e.g., BSA/AML act amendments).
adapter_notes: Check for RSS feed at www.fincen.gov/news/rss or similar. If no RSS, scrape news page HTML. No auth required. Poll daily for new items. Categorize by type (advisory, enforcement, ruling).
verify: Confirm whether RSS feed exists or if HTML scraping is needed. Check historical archive depth.

name: FATF Public Statements and News
contamination_reasoning: International standards body issuing public statements on high-risk jurisdictions and AML/CFT compliance. Future statements don't exist yet. Very unlikely to be in any TSFM pretraining corpus — obscure, event-driven, and domain-specific.
difficulty_note: Statements are clustered around plenary meetings (3x/year) with irregular intersessional outputs. Jurisdiction risk-rating changes (blacklist/greylist additions/removals) are rare, high-impact events that create non-stationary patterns.
adapter_notes: Scrape publications page HTML. No API or RSS confirmed. Parse for publication date, title, and type. Poll weekly. May need to handle JavaScript-rendered content.
verify: Confirm page structure is scrapable. Check if RSS feed exists at an alternate URL. Verify historical archive availability.

name: DOJ Criminal Fraud Section Press Releases
contamination_reasoning: DOJ Fraud Section press releases on criminal fraud cases. Different from SEC enforcement (already wired). Future cases don't exist yet. Event-driven legal data, not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: Case announcement rate varies with DOJ priorities, political administration changes, and major fraud schemes (e.g., PPP fraud surge in 2020-2022). Non-stationary with regime shifts.
adapter_notes: Check for RSS feed at www.justice.gov/feeds/ or scrape press release listings. No auth required. Parse for date, case type (securities fraud, bank fraud, healthcare fraud, etc.). Poll daily.
verify: Confirm RSS URL or scrape endpoint. Check if press releases are categorized by fraud type.

name: Avalanche.org Incident and Forecast Data
estimated_series_count: 2-4 (avalanche count by region, danger rating by forecast zone)
contamination_reasoning: Avalanche incident reports and forecasts from US avalanche centers. Obscure domain, not a standard TSFM dataset. Future avalanches don't exist yet. Event-driven natural hazard data.
difficulty_note: Strong seasonal pattern (winter only) with high inter-annual variability driven by snowpack conditions. Within-season, avalanche frequency depends on storm cycles and temperature swings — not trivially periodic.
adapter_notes: Scrape avalanche.org for incident reports and forecast zone danger ratings. May have JSON endpoints for forecast data. No auth required. Poll daily during winter season. Check for API at avalanche.org/api or similar.
verify: Confirm whether structured API exists or HTML scraping is needed. Check historical archive depth and regional coverage.

name: RubyGems.org Version Release API
estimated_series_count: 100+ (top gems by download count; version release events per gem)
contamination_reasoning: Ruby package registry — different ecosystem from NPM, PyPI, and crates.io (all wired). Version release timestamps are event-driven and future releases don't exist yet. While RubyGems is easy to find, the specific time series of release events is not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: Release cadence varies enormously by gem: some have regular monthly release cycles, others are sporadic with multi-year gaps. Security advisory-driven patch releases create correlated bursts across the ecosystem. Models must handle heterogeneous inter-arrival times.
adapter_notes: REST API at rubygems.org/api/v1. No auth required for public data. Use /api/v1/gems/{name}/versions.json for version timestamps and /api/v1/downloads for download stats. Rate limit: ~10 req/s. Poll daily for new releases.
verify: Confirm API endpoints and rate limits. Check historical version data availability.

name: BoardGameGeek XML API v2
license: Open (BGG, data available for personal/non-commercial use)
contamination_reasoning: Board game community platform. Very obscure relative to NPM/Wikimedia/etc. Future user activity doesn't exist yet. Not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: Rating/collection activity is driven by holiday gift seasons (November-December spikes), board game convention cycles (Gen Con, Essen Spiel), and Kickstarter campaign fulfillment waves. Non-trivial seasonal + event-driven hybrid.
adapter_notes: XML API v2 at api.boardgamegeek.com/xmlapi2. No auth required. Use /things?id={id}&stats=1 for game stats and /forumlist?id={id} for forum activity. Rate limit: 1 req/s. Poll daily for new ratings/collections.
verify: Confirm API supports historical timestamp extraction for ratings/collections. Check rate limits.

name: EU Clinical Trials Register XML API
estimated_series_count: 1-3 (trial registration count, trial status change count, phase distribution)
contamination_reasoning: European clinical trials register. Different from ClinicalTrials.gov (already proposed). Future trial registrations don't exist yet. Event-driven regulatory data, not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: Registration rate varies with therapeutic area trends, regulatory deadlines (e.g., EU CTR deadline transitions), and public health emergencies (COVID-19 surge in 2020). Non-stationary with regime shifts from regulatory changes.
adapter_notes: XML API at www.clinicaltrialsregister.eu/ctr/search/search?query={query}&resultType=CORE. No auth required. Parse XML for trial registration date, status, phase. Poll daily for new registrations. May need to handle pagination.
verify: Confirm XML API endpoint and response format. Check pagination and historical data depth.

name: CEPS Czech Republic Grid Data Portal
estimated_series_count: 10-20 (load, generation by type, frequency, cross-border flows)
contamination_reasoning: Czech Republic transmission system operator. Not among the well-known ISO/RTOs already covered (AEMO, CAISO, NYISO, BPA, Elia, Energinet, etc.). Regional TSO data is unlikely to be in pretraining corpora. Future grid data doesn't exist yet.
difficulty_note: Grid load has multi-seasonal patterns (daily, weekly, annual), weather dependence, and cross-border flow dynamics with neighboring TSOs (German, Slovak, Polish). Solar/wind generation adds intermittent volatility. Not trivially periodic.
adapter_notes: Check www.ceps.cz/en/data for API or downloadable data. May use CSV/XML downloads or a REST API. No auth expected for open data. Check for real-time generation mix and load data. Poll every 1-5 min.
verify: Confirm API endpoint or data download URL. Check data granularity (1-min vs 5-min vs 15-min). Verify historical data availability.

name: CBP Border Wait Times
contamination_reasoning: US Customs and Border Protection border wait time data. Not a standard TSFM dataset. Future wait times don't exist yet. No existing transport source covers border crossings.
difficulty_note: Wait times have strong daily/weekly seasonality (commuter rush hours, weekend shopping), holiday-driven spikes (Thanksgiving, Christmas, summer), and event-driven disruptions (border policy changes, security incidents). Seasonal-naive would miss holiday surges and policy-driven regime shifts.
adapter_notes: Scrape apps.cbp.gov/bwt/ for port wait times. Data updated approximately every 30-60 min. No API confirmed — may need HTML parsing. Extract port name, wait time (minutes), lane type (passenger, commercial, SENTRI). Poll every 30 min.
verify: Confirm whether structured API exists or HTML scraping is needed. Check update frequency and historical data availability.

name: Ofgem Enforcement Actions
license: Open (UK government regulator, Open Government Licence)
contamination_reasoning: UK energy regulator enforcement actions. Different from grid operators (which provide operational data). Future enforcement actions don't exist yet. Obscure regulatory data, not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: Enforcement action rate is driven by regulatory priority changes, energy market crises (e.g., 2022 UK energy price cap crisis), and consumer complaint volumes. Non-stationary with regime shifts following major market events.
adapter_notes: Scrape www.ofgem.gov.uk/enforcement for action listings. No API confirmed. Parse for date, case type, penalty amount, and company. Poll weekly. May need to handle JavaScript-rendered content.
verify: Confirm page is scrapable. Check historical archive depth. Verify if RSS feed exists.

name: CDC HAN (Health Alert Network) RSS Feed
estimated_series_count: 1-3 (alert counts by category: Health Advisory, Health Update, Info Message)
contamination_reasoning: Live RSS feed of health alerts issued in real time. Future alerts don't exist yet. Historical alerts are obscure enough (not a standard TSFM dataset) and the feed is event-driven, not a clean multivariate time series that would be included in pretraining.
difficulty_note: Event-driven alerts with regime-switching: pandemic periods produce bursts of advisories, while inter-pandemic periods are sparse. Models must capture non-stationary event rates, not just seasonality.
adapter_notes: RSS/XML feed at emergency.cdc.gov/han/rss/. No auth required. Parse <item> elements for pubDate and category. Poll every 15-30 min for new items. Aggregate to daily event counts for benchmarking.
verify: Confirm RSS endpoint URL returns valid XML. Check that alert categories are consistently tagged.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-04 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: USDA FSIS Food Safety Recalls RSS
contamination_reasoning: Live RSS feed of meat/poultry/egg product recalls. Different from OpenFDA (which covers FDA-regulated foods, not USDA-regulated meats). Future recalls don't exist yet. Not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: Recalls are event-driven with seasonal clustering (summer heat increases foodborne illness), outbreak-driven bursts, and varying recall sizes (Class I vs II vs III). A seasonal-naive baseline would miss outbreak-driven spikes.
adapter_notes: RSS feed available at www.fsis.usda.gov/recalls/rss. No auth required. Parse for recall date, product type, recall class, and pounds recalled. Poll hourly.
verify: Confirm RSS URL. Check historical archive availability for backfilling.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-05 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: AuroraWatch UK Realtime Alert API
estimated_series_count: 2-3 (alert level, magnetic field strength, alert count)
contamination_reasoning: UK academic project using magnetometers in the UK. Different from NOAA SWPC (which uses US data). Obscure enough to not be in pretraining corpora. Future aurora alerts don't exist yet.
difficulty_note: Alert frequency depends on solar activity (11-year solar cycle), geomagnetic storms, and local weather conditions. Multi-seasonal with long-period solar cycle modulation and short-period storm-driven bursts. Strong models must capture both.
adapter_notes: REST API at aurorawatch.lancs.ac.uk/api/. No auth required. Returns JSON with alert status, magnetic field readings, and timestamps. Poll every 1-5 min for real-time data. Historical data available via API.
verify: Confirm API endpoint and response format. Check historical data availability and date range.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-04 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: AniList GraphQL API Activity Stream
license: Open (AniList, data available for non-commercial use)
estimated_series_count: 50-200 (activity counts per anime/manga series; trending score)
contamination_reasoning: Anime/manga tracking platform with user activity data. Obscure relative to Wikipedia/Wikimedia (wired). Future user activity doesn't exist yet. Not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: Activity is driven by anime seasonal broadcast cycles (3-4 cour seasons per year), manga chapter releases, and viral events. Strong seasonal pattern with irregular spikes from unexpected hit series. Models must capture both broadcast seasonality and viral event dynamics.
adapter_notes: GraphQL API at graphql.anilist.co. No auth required for public queries (rate-limited). Query MediaListCollection, Page.media, and Activity for timestamps. Poll hourly for trending. Use pagination for historical data.
verify: Confirm GraphQL schema supports time-series extraction. Check rate limits and pagination depth.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-06 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: RIPE RIS (Routing Information Service) Live BGP Stream
estimated_series_count: 50-100 (BGP update counts per collector/peer, route announcement/withdrawal events)
estimated_length: ~20+ years of BGP data (RIS has been running since ~1999)
contamination_reasoning: Real-time BGP routing update stream from RIPE NCC collectors. Different from RIPEstat (wired, which provides aggregate stats). Raw BGP updates are event-driven and future updates don't exist yet. Not a standard TSFM dataset.
difficulty_note: BGP update volume is driven by network outages, route hijacks, misconfigurations, and maintenance windows. Bursty, non-stationary, with heavy-tailed event sizes. A seasonal-naive baseline would be useless. Models must capture event-driven dynamics.
adapter_notes: WebSocket stream at ris-live.ripe.net/v1/stream/?format=json. No auth required. Subscribe to BGP update messages. Filter by collector (e.g., RRC00, RRC01). Aggregate to per-minute or per-half-hour update counts for benchmarking. Historical data available via RIS raw data (MRT format).
verify: Confirm WebSocket endpoint and message format. Check historical data access via MRT files at ris.ripe.net.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-04 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source
